headline_drivers: Q1 2026 earnings shock on 2026-05-06: consolidated net revenue rose 114% year over year to about $197M, with EPS swinging to $0.13 from -$0.53, driving a sharp re-rating

Advertising Technology & Services revenue surged 204% year over year to about $154.6M-$155M in Q1 2026, with operating profit rising to $34.3M from $6.5M and monthly active advertisers / revenue per advertiser both higher
Management flagged a strong 2026 political ad cycle on the 2026-05-06 earnings call, citing Election Day 2026 being 182 days away and ongoing primaries as a catalyst for higher political spend
The board declared a $0.05 quarterly cash dividend payable 2026-06-30 (record 2026-06-16), reinforcing the turnaround / cash flow narrative
summary: The market is reacting primarily to Entravision’s blowout Q1 2026 earnings: revenue more than doubled, EPS flipped from a large loss to a profit, and the high-growth Advertising Technology & Services segment posted triple-digit growth with improved profitability. Management also pointed to an approaching 2026 political ad cycle as an additional upside catalyst. I did not find evidence of a separate named-customer win or major M&A/regulatory event driving the move; this looks like an earnings-driven repricing with momentum following the surprise.
